product_surfaces: internal/cache.SchemaVersionError typed error with compatibility diagnostic
internal/cache.MigrateCache() compatibility pre-check and migration dispatch
evidence_type: offline executable runtime validation through Go tests generated by run.sh, exercising production internal/cache package directly
freshness: current repository checkout; validation exercises production internal/cache package behavior against all three schema compatibility states (current, older/v2/compatible, older/v1/incompatible, future/incompatible)

mocks_used.name: temp filesystem
mocks_used.purpose: create temporary SQLite database files for deterministic schema version testing

product_gaps.description: NewSQLiteReadOnlyStore at internal/cache/sqlite.go:136-138 gates on !compat.PermitWrites, which rejects version-2 caches even though Compatible==true. A read-only store only needs read access, so PermitWrites==false should not block read-only access. This prevents the doctor command (which uses NewSQLiteReadOnlyStore via openReadOnlyStore) from reporting schema version diagnostics on caches that need migration. The CheckVersionCompatibility correctly returns Compatible=true, PermitWrites=false for version-2 — it is the read-only store's gating logic that is overly restrictive.
product_gaps.fix_hint: In NewSQLiteReadOnlyStore, check Compatible before PermitWrites: if !compat.Compatible, block with SchemaVersionError; if !compat.PermitWrites, allow read-only access with a warning. Only NewSQLiteStore (read-write) should block on PermitWrites.

product_gaps.description: NewSQLiteReadOnlyStore blocks on !compat.PermitWrites for iter-1 (pre-schema-versioning) caches. While these caches are truly incompatible (Compatible=false), the doctor command cannot report the incompatibility diagnostic — it just shows cache status 'not_available' without the actionable re-initialization message. The SchemaVersionError carrying the compatibility struct should be surfaced through the doctor report.
product_gaps.fix_hint: In executeDoctorCommand, check for ErrSchemaVersionIncompatible in the storeErr path and extract the SchemaVersionError.Compat to populate a cache schema compatibility section in the doctor report.

product_gaps.description: MigrateCache does not validate that initial schema tables exist before running downstream migrations. On a corrupted iter-2 cache (schema_version=2 but tables from v1/v2 missing), ApplyChunkPolicyMigration fails with 'no such table: chunks' instead of a graceful diagnostic. This is a synthetic scenario — a real iter-2 cache reaches v2 through the full migration sequence and always has all tables. Not reachable under normal product operation.
product_gaps.fix_hint: Add a pre-migration validation step that checks for the existence of required tables before running individual migration apply functions.

notes: All validation is offline and deterministic — no network access, no OS keychain, no GitCode API calls.

The implementation of CheckVersionCompatibility at internal/cache/schema.go correctly produces the expected diagnostics for all three schema states (current, older/compatible, older/incompatible, future).

The product gap gap-012-v2-readonly-blocked is the primary issue: it prevents diagnostic tools from reporting schema version information on caches that need migration, which contradicts the acceptance criteria requiring that the schema version mismatch be 'reported'.
Existing internal/cache unit tests all pass, confirming no regression from this task's changes.
The MigrateCache command (task 005 / 013) was implemented separately and correctly handles the version-2 migration path when run against a fully-formed iter-2 cache.
